question: Calculate the average weight of all Pico Rivera shipments.
orig_question: Calculate the average height of all neutral superheroes.
orig_masked: Calculate the average [C23] of all [V1] [T9]. ; [V1] is a value of the column [C1]
gold_sql: SELECT AVG(T1.weight) FROM shipment as T1 INNER JOIN city as T2 ON T1.city_id=T2.city_id WHERE T2.city_name = 'Pico Rivera'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT AVG(T1.height_cm) FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN alignment AS T2 ON T1.alignment_id = T2.id WHERE T2.alignment = 'Neutral'

question: List the genre names of the movie named Finding Nemo.
orig_question: List the attribute value of the superhero named Aquababy.
orig_masked: List the [C10] of the [T9] [C28] [V1]. ; [V1] is a value of the column [C28]
gold_sql: SELECT T3.genre_name FROM movie as T1 INNER JOIN movie_genres as T2 ON T1.movie_id = T2.movie_id INNER JOIN genre as T3 ON T2.genre_id = T3.genre_id WHERE T1.title = 'Finding Nemo'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T2.attribute_value FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN hero_attribute AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.hero_id WHERE T1.superhero_name = 'Aquababy'

question: Please list athlete names which are higher than 180. height>180;
orig_question: Please list player names which are higher than 180. height>180;
orig_masked: Please list [T4] [C126] which are higher than [V1]. [C122]>[V1];; [V1] is a value of the column [C122]
gold_sql: SELECT full_name FROM Person WHERE height > 180
orig_gold_sql: SELECT player_name FROM Player WHERE height > 180

question: Who is the technical advisor of the episode with the largest votes?
orig_question: Who is the owner of the account with the largest loan amount?
orig_masked: Who is the [V1] of the [T1] with the largest [C34]?  ; [V1] is a value of the column [C16]
gold_sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Person as T1 INNER JOIN Credit as T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.person_id INNER JOIN Episode as T3 ON T3.episode_id = T2.episode_id WHERE T2.role = 'technical advisor' ORDER BY T3.votes DESC LIMIT 1
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T1.client_id FROM disp AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T3 ON T1.account_id = T3.account_id INNER JOIN loan AS T2 ON T3.account_id = T2.account_id WHERE T1.type = 'OWNER' ORDER BY T2.amount DESC LIMIT 1

question: What types of violations does the business with the id 10 own?
orig_question: What type of credit card does the client with the id 13539 own?
orig_masked: What [C8] of [T2] does the [T3] with the [C10] [V1] own?  ; [V1] is a value of the column [C10]
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.violation_type_id FROM violations as T1 INNER JOIN businesses as T2 ON T1.business_id = T2.business_id WHERE T2.business_id = 10
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T3.type FROM disp AS T1 INNER JOIN client AS T2 ON T1.client_id = T2.client_id INNER JOIN card AS T3 ON T1.disp_id = T3.disp_id WHERE T2.client_id = 13539
note: I removed the own link. I don't think it makes sense?

question: Which athlete is the tallest? tallest athlete refers to MAX(height);
orig_question: Which player is the tallest? tallest player refers to MAX(height);
orig_masked: Which [T4] is the [C122]? [C122] [T4] refers to MAX([C122]);
gold_sql: SELECT full_name FROM Person ORDER BY height DESC LIMIT 1
orig_gold_sql: SELECT player_name FROM Player ORDER BY height DESC LIMIT 1

question: List out the titles who have the earliest publication date in 1995 ?
orig_question: List out the accounts who have the earliest trading date in 1995 ?
orig_masked: List out the [C47] who have the [C51] [C51] in [V1] ? ; [V1] is a value of the column [C51]
gold_sql: SELECT title FROM book WHERE publication_date = (SELECT publication_date from book WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', publication_date) = '1995' ORDER BY publication_date ASC LIMIT 1)
orig_gold_sql: SELECT account_id FROM trans WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', date) = '1995' ORDER BY date ASC LIMIT 1

question: Please list all the orders made by customers from region South.
orig_question: Please list all the transactions made by accounts from district 5.
orig_masked: Please list all the [T8] made by [T1] from [T5] [V1]. ; [V1] is a value of the column [C32]
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.OrderNumber FROM "Sales Orders" AS T1 INNER JOIN "Store Locations" as T2 ON T1._StoreID = T2.StoreID INNER JOIN Regions as T3 ON T2.StateCode = T3.StateCode WHERE T3.Region = 'South'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T3.trans_id FROM district AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id INNER JOIN trans AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T1.district_id = 5
note: Removed the made link as I don't think it makes sense?

question: Which customers have a shipment greater than 17,500 in 2016?
orig_question: Which districts have transactions greater than USS$10,000 in 1997?
orig_masked: Which [T5] have [T8] greater than [V1] in [V2]? ; [V1] is a value of the column [C48]; [V2] is a value of the column [C51]
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.cust_name FROM customer as T1 INNER JOIN shipment as T2 ON T1.cust_id = T2.cust_id WHERE T2.weight > 17500 and STRFTIME('%Y', T2.ship_date) = '2016'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T1.district_id FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id INNER JOIN trans AS T3 ON T1.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', T3.date) = '1997' GROUP BY T1.district_id HAVING SUM(T3.amount) > 10000

question: Give the publisher ID of What Life Was Like in the Jewel in the Crown: British India  AD 1600-1905.  What Life Was Like in the Jewel in the Crown: British India  AD 1600-1905 is the publisher_name;
orig_question: Give the publisher ID of Star Trek.  Star Trek is the publisher_name;
orig_masked: Give the [T7] [C14] of [V1].  [V1] is the [C15];; [V1] is a value of the column [C15]
evidence: What Life Was Like in the Jewel in the Crown: British India  AD 1600-1905 is the publisher_name;
gold_sql: SELECT T1.publisher_id FROM publisher as T1 INNER JOIN book as T2 ON T1.publisher_id = T2.publisher_id WHERE T2.title = 'What Life Was Like in the Jewel in the Crown: British India  AD 1600-1905'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT id FROM publisher WHERE publisher_name = 'Star Trek'
note: I don't have the original database, but I don't think the value link for Star Trek is correct here? That's a title/series not a publisher?
